intro: The most concrete split between Chargebee and Iugu is the pricing model: Chargebee runs on hybrid pricing from $599/mo (Performance), with a free tier, while Iugu uses usage-based pricing. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Chargebee carries medium lock-in (medium — deep integrations, underandwithon logic) and hybrid pricing from $599/mo (Performance), with a free tier; Iugu carries medium lock-in (brazil billing platform) and usage-based pricing. Chargebee is a fit when you are saas. Iugu is a fit when you are brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance. The honest trade-off: Chargebee is explicitly not for simple projects; Iugu is explicitly not for global-first saas or companies without significant brazilian revenue exposure. On the product side, Chargebee is described as Subscription and revenue management platform supporting SaaS, e-commerce, and box subscriptions with Stripe and Braintree, and Iugu is described as Brazilian billing and payments platform for subscriptions, recurring charges, and marketplaces with REST API.
quick_take: Chargebee is for saas; Iugu is for brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Chargebee if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches saas.
verdict_b: Choose Iugu if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Chargebee's hybrid model, and you are brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance.

faq.a: Chargebee has a free tier; Iugu does not. For small-scale use Chargebee will be cheaper by definition. At paid scale you need to model your usage against each vendor's plans.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Chargebee to Iugu?
faq.a: Migration from Chargebee to Iugu is feasible but not trivial: Chargebee has medium lock-in (medium — deep integrations, underandwithon logic). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Chargebee rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Iugu. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in Chargebee. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Iugu a good alternative to Chargebee?
faq.a: Iugu is pitched at brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance, which overlaps with Chargebee's fit (saas) but is not identical. Note that Iugu is explicitly not for global-first saas or companies without significant brazilian revenue exposure, so it only replaces Chargebee if you are outside that exclusion. Pricing models differ (hybrid vs usage), which may shift total cost.
